Title: The pathway of melatonin biosynthesis in common wheat (Triticum aestivum)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Multifunctional plant biomolecule implicated in alleviation of adverse environmental conditions such as UV exposure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates tryptophan to produce tryptamine; also converts 5-hydroxytryptophan to serotonin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early precursor pool for melatonin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream committed-step enzyme; highest activity among the four pathway enzymes discussed in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tryptamine accumulation and supports melatonin pathway flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein from Escherichia coli; kinetic assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS associated the Trm locus with SNP AX-03551850 and identified TraesCS2D01G518200 within the LD block. 2. Purified recombinant protein catalyzed Trp-to-Trm conversion and also converted 5OH-Trp to Ser. 3. The gene was validated as a wheat TDC contributing to melatonin-pathway precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling two-leaf stage used for mGWAS profiling; greenhouse and field-grown tissues profiled
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream structural gene for engineering melatonin biosynthesis, but likely functional redundancy in hexaploid wheat may weaken downstream metabolite gains from manipulating a single TDC copy
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Coding product showed similar enzymatic properties to TraesCS2D01G518300, with Km 148 μM and kcat 6.08/s for Trp. Source gene identified by forward genetics.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS metabolomics) of six pathway intermediates; metabolite-GWAS using wheat resequencing-derived SNPs (unpublished resequencing data)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Multifunctional plant biomolecule implicated in alleviation of adverse environmental conditions such as UV exposure
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms early precursor pool for melatonin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream committed-step enzyme; highest activity among the four pathway enzymes discussed in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression elevates tryptamine content in wheat leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein from Escherichia coli; kinetic assay; transgenic wheat overexpression with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS associated Trm variation with the locus containing TraesCS2D01G518300. 2. Recombinant protein catalyzed Trp-to-Trm conversion with similar properties to TraesCS2D01G518200. 3. Overexpression in wheat increased Trm content, validating its function in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling two-leaf stage used for mGWAS profiling; greenhouse and field-grown tissues profiled
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression elevated Trm but caused only slight or no increase in Ser, likely due to functional redundancy in hexaploid wheat
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for increasing melatonin precursor supply, but single-gene overexpression may not substantially elevate downstream melatonin because of redundancy and downstream bottlenecks
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Km 150 μM and kcat 4.47/s for Trp. Also reported generally similar to other plant TDCs; preliminary experiments indicated similar enzymatic properties to TraesCS2D01G518200.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS metabolomics) of six pathway intermediates; metabolite-GWAS using wheat resequencing-derived SNPs (unpublished resequencing data)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Multifunctional plant biomolecule implicated in stress alleviation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct intermediate en route to N-acetylserotonin and melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for serotonin accumulation at the mapped locus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes serotonin accumulation; a 463-bp insertion variant is associated with lower serotonin content and likely impairs activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; transient coexpression in tobacco with wheat TDC; haplotype/sequence variation analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS linked serotonin variation to the locus containing TraesCS4B01G396300. 2. Coexpression assays in tobacco showed the wheat gene converts Trm to Ser, although at lower activity than rice T5H. 3. A 463-bp insertion in the first exon was strongly associated with lower Ser content and likely reduced enzyme function.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling two-leaf stage used for mGWAS profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Variant allele with 463-bp insertion likely reduces catalytic efficiency and serotonin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for haplotype selection or editing to enhance serotonin and downstream melatonin biosynthesis in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct in vitro assay using E. coli-expressed protein from Chinese Spring reference sequence showed no hydroxylation activity, but tobacco coexpression with TDC demonstrated T5H function. Wheat activity lower than rice LOC_Os12g16720.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS metabolomics) of six pathway intermediates; metabolite-GWAS using wheat resequencing-derived SNPs (unpublished resequencing data)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Multifunctional plant biomolecule implicated in stress alleviation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates serotonin to produce N-acetylserotonin; can also acetylate 5-methoxytryptamine to melatonin with lower efficiency
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor NAS for melatonin; can also directly form melatonin from 5M-Trm
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme controlling NAS accumulation at the mapped locus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression elevates NAS content in wheat; contributes to melatonin biosynthesis through two possible reaction routes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein from Escherichia coli; kinetic assay; transgenic wheat overexpression with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. mGWAS identified TraesCS1B01G312700 as the candidate for NAS variation. 2. Recombinant protein converted Ser to NAS and also 5M-Trm to Mel. 3. Overexpression in wheat increased NAS content, validating its in vivo contribution.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling two-leaf stage used for mGWAS profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NAS increased in overexpression lines, but no detectable melatonin increase was observed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target for boosting NAS and potentially melatonin, but may need stacking with downstream or parallel-pathway genes to raise final melatonin
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Km 618.9 μM and kcat 1.89 × 10^-6/s for Ser-to-NAS reaction; for 5M-Trm-to-Mel reaction Km >1000 μM. SNAT activity toward Trm and tyramine was also tested.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS metabolomics) of six pathway intermediates; metabolite-GWAS using wheat resequencing-derived SNPs (unpublished resequencing data)

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Multifunctional plant biomolecule implicated in stress alleviation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal methylation branch; supports both Ser→5M-Trm and NAS→Mel routes
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates serotonin to form 5-methoxytryptamine and methylates N-acetylserotonin to form melatonin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms melatonin from NAS and also forms alternative precursor 5M-Trm from Ser
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal pathway enzyme; not established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes final methylation steps needed to complete the wheat melatonin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based candidate identification; in vitro enzyme assay with recombinant protein from Escherichia coli; kinetic assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. No ASMT/COMT gene was recovered by mGWAS, so TraesCS1D01G016700 was identified by homology to rice ASMT genes. 2. Recombinant protein converted Ser to 5M-Trm and NAS to Mel. 3. This validation completed construction of the wheat melatonin biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important downstream gene for completing melatonin engineering, especially in gene-stacking strategies with TDC, T5H, and SNAT
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Kinetic parameters: Ser-to-5M-Trm Km 70.35 μM, kcat 8.38 × 10^-6/s; NAS-to-Mel Km 457 μM, kcat 2.30 × 10^-3/s. Identified by reverse genetics rather than mGWAS.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite profiling (LC-MS/MS metabolomics); phylogenetic/homology analysis against rice ASMT1/2/3
